categoryDescription: Configure the FileVault Options payload to customize FileVault disk encryption settings on devices.

Configure the Login Window Behavior payload to set preferences for user login, control the user’s ability to restart and shut down devices from the login window, and set the appearance of the login window.
Configure the Finder payload to control Finder preference settings and specify which commands can be used on devices.
Configure the Xsan payload to configure an Xsan client system.

description: Declarative Device Management preview Math Settings
App Management > Autonomous Single App Mode
categoryDescription: Configure the ASAM payload to set an app to control devices. Installing this payload on a device grants low-level access to the system for the specified app - which includes, but is not limited to, installing systemwide monitoring capabilities.
Use the App Store payload to configure macOS App Store restrictions on devices.

description: Media Management > Media Management Disc Burning
categoryDescription: Configure the Media Management Disc Burning payload to control disc-burning settings on devices.
Configure the User interface preference settings to manage how users can interact with the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint user interface.
Configure the Parental Controls Dictionary payload to control dictionary and dictation content on macOS devices.
Configure the Privacy Preferences Policy Control payload to manage the settings in the Privacy pane of Security & Privacy preferences on devices.
Configure the Security Preferences payload to set various security options on devices.
Configure the File Provider payload to control which file providers can be used on an enrolled device.
Configure the System Policy Managed payload to configure the Finder's contextual menu to bypass the system policy.
Configure the Cloud-delivered protection preference settings to manage the cloud-driven protection features of Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.
Configure the Antivirus engine settings to manage the preferences of the antivirus component of Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.

Configure the Microsoft Office preference settings to control diagnostic data that is collected and sent to Microsoft about Office client software being used as well as connected experiences that use cloud-based functionality to provide enhanced Office features to you and your users.
Configure the User Preferences payload to manage iCloud password preferences for local accounts on devices.
AirPrint is an Apple feature that is built into most printer models and allows devices to print files over a wireless network. Use these settings to add AirPrint information to devices.
Configure the Time Machine payload to define the location and options for Time Machine backups on managed devices.

description: Microsoft Edge Edge Website Typo Protection settings
Microsoft Edge\Startup, home page and new tab page
displayName: Startup, home page and new tab page

categoryDescription: Configure the Calendar payload to provide account settings for connecting to a CalDAV-compliant calendar server. As with Exchange accounts, users need to manually enter information you omit from the profile, such as their account password, when the profile is installed.

description: Authentication > Extensible Single Sign On Kerberos
categoryDescription: The Kerberos Extensible SSO uses Apple's built-in Kerberos extension. This option is a Kerberos-specific version of the Credential app extension.
Configure the Microsoft Outlook preference settings to set a default preference which users can change or to force a particular preference which users can’t change.

Configure the Content Caching payload to configure devices to save a local copy of content, making it easier to be retrieved by other Apple devices without connecting to the Internet. This caching accelerates downloads by saving software updates, apps, photos, and other content the first time they're downloaded. Since apps are downloaded once and shared to other devices, schools and organizations with many devices save bandwidth.
Configure the Printing payload to manage printer settings for devices, such as specifying which printers are configured for use and applying a footer.
Configure the Screensaver payload to manage screensaver settings on enrolled devices.

Configure the Software Update payload to control whether macOS devices check for and install updates automatically, can install prerelease software, and more. These settings also let you set client options and prevent end users from making changes to configured settings.
The managed settings allows the server to set settings on the device. These settings take effect on a one-time basis. The user may still be able to change the settings at a later time.

description: Parental Controls > Parental Controls Content Filter
categoryDescription: Configure the Parental Controls Content Filter payload to control access to content on macOS devices.

Configure the Energy Saver payload to configure power-related settings for enrolled devices. Some hardware-specific options must be set manually on the device and aren’t available through a configuration profile.
Configure the Screensaver User payload to manage a user’s screensaver settings on devices.
Configure the Global Preferences payload to manage global settings on devices such as multiple sessions and auto logout delay.
Use the Mobile Account payload to configure mobile account creation and related settings on devices.
Configure the Associated Domains payload to manage domains with features such as Extensible App SSO, universal links, and Password Auto Fill for devices.
Configure the FileVault payload to manage FileVault disk encryption settings on devices.
Configure the FileVault Recovery Key Escrow payload to customize FileVault Recovery Key Escrow settings on devices.

description: Microsoft Defender > Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) preferences
categoryDescription: Configure the Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) preference settings to manage the EDR component of Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.
Configure the Firewall payload to turn on the Firewall in macOS to prevent unwanted connections from the internet or other networks.
Configure the Dock payload to configure the dock on devices.

description: Microsoft Edge\ Private Network Request Settings
Declarative Device Management preview Software Update Settings

categoryDescription: Configure the Login Window Behavior payload to specify the apps, files, folders, and network mounts that are opened when users log in.

Configure the Profile Removal Password payload to provide a password to allow users to remove a locked configuration profile from the device. If this payload is present and has a password value set, the device asks for the password when the user taps a profile's Remove button. Profiles are only able to be removed if configured as removable. This payload is encrypted with the rest of the profile.
Configure the Network Proxy Configuration settings to permit the device to use a proxy server for access to websites.
Configure the System Logging payload to manage private data logging on devices.
Configure the Accessibility payload to define specific settings for users who have difficulty with vision, hearing, or physical mobility.

description: Declarative Device Management preview Software Update Enforce Latest
Declarative Device Management preview Extension Settings

categoryDescription: Configure the Contacts payload to provide account settings for connecting to a CardDAV-compliant contact server. If you omit the account information, users need to enter it manually when the profile is installed.
Configure the Login Items payload to specify the apps, files, folders, and network mounts that are opened when users log in.

Configure the System Extensions payload settings for enrolled devices. Installing or removing this payload can change the state of system extensions on the Mac. If a containing app activates a system extension, and the system extension is in a pending state, installing a payload that allows the extension completes the activation process. If a system extension is active, removing a payload that allows the extension deactivates that extension.
Configure the Desktop payload to configure the desktop on devices.
Use the NS Extension Management payload to control which extensions can be used on a device.
Configure the DNS Settings payload to control DNS settings for on devices, such as specifying apps that must use specific DNS settings.

description: Parental Controls > Parental Controls Game Center
categoryDescription: Configure the Parental Controls Game Center payload to control GameKit feature account modification.
Configure the System Preferences payload to manage the panes in the System Preferences user interface on devices.

Configure the Web Content Filter payload configure devices to use Apple’s built-in filter or connect and authenticate to a 3rd party content filter provider service. You can create a list of allowed web links and restricted web links. For example, you can allow only contoso web sites to open.

description: Declarative Device Management preview Disk Management

categoryDescription: Configure the Directory Service payload to add directory servers and settings to devices.
Configure the Accounts payload to enable or disable guest accounts.

These settings configure the declarations used by Apple’s declarative device management feature. These settings are separate from older MDM settings and only apply to a device enabled for declarative management. Learn more about declarative management at developer.apple.com

description: Declarative Device Management preview Safari Browser

categoryDescription: Configure the Domains payload to specify marked domains, which mail domains are marked in Mail on the device, and which web domains’ documents are considered managed in iOS and iPadOS.

description: Parental Controls > Parental Controls Time Limits
categoryDescription: Configure the Parental Controls Time Limits payload to limit the amount of daily device use and restrict the hours when the device can be used.
Configure the Notification payload to manage notifications for specific applications, such as blocking notification previews or controlling how notifications appear when devices are unlocked.
Configure the Xsan Preferences payload to configure which volumes automatically mount at startup.

description: Microsoft Edge\ Permit or deny screen capture

categoryDescription: Configure the Bluetooth command to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices. Users will still be able to modify Bluetooth on their device even if configured by this command. It is recommended to set the Allow Bluetooth Modification in Restrictions to False to prevent users from modifying the Bluetooth value that is configured by this command.
Configure the AirPlay payload to define AirPlay settings on devices, such as preconfiguring passwords for AirPlay Mirroring destinations and to listing allowed destinations for devices.
Configure the Identification payload to save names of the account user and prompt text on devices. If the username is left blank, users must provide this information when installing the profile.

description: Login > Service Management - Managed Login Items
categoryDescription: Configure the Service Management – Managed Login Items payload to manage the background tasks and items that are opened when users log in.
Configure the Smart Card payload to manage specific settings for Smart Cards on enrolled devices. User overrides aren’t permitted.
Configure the Managed Menu Extras payload to control the menu extras on devices.
Configure the Microsoft AutoUpdate preferences to control the update process for Microsoft applications on devices.

Configure the Global HTTP Proxy payload to specify a proxy for all HTTP traffic to and from enrolled devices. If you choose Manual proxy type you need the proxy server address — including its port and, optionally, a username and password for logging in to the proxy server. If you choose Auto proxy type, you can enter a proxy auto-configuration (PAC) URL.

Configure the Passcode payload to specify whether a password or passcode is required to access and use an enrolled device. When the configuration profile is installed, users are asked to enter a password or passcode that meets the policies you specify. Otherwise, the profile won’t be installed. When the Passcode payload is installed on an iPhone or iPad, users have 60 minutes to enter a passcode. If users don’t do so within that time frame, the payload forces them to enter a passcode using the specified settings. If you use device policies and Exchange passcode policies, the two sets of policies are merged and the strictest settings are enforced.
Configure the Time Server payload to manage time server settings for devices.

description: Microsoft Edge Related Website Sets Settings

categoryDescription: Use the LDAP payload to enter settings to enable corporate directory services for users such as connecting to an LDAPv3 directory.
Configure an app extension that enables single sign-on (SSO) for devices.

description: Parental Controls > Parental Controls Application Restrictions
categoryDescription: Configure the Parental Controls Application Restrictions payload to restrict applications on macOS devices.

Configure the Restrictions payload to enable or disable features on devices. These configurations can be used prevent users from accessing a specific app, service or function on enrolled devices. For example, a restriction can be added that prevents an iPhone or iPad from using AirPrint. Another restriction can be added to prevent the sharing of passwords over AirDrop on an iPhone, iPad and Mac. Certain restrictions on an iPhone may be mirrored on a paired Apple Watch.
